Xbox Network Configuration Tool (XNetConfig)

The XNetConfig tool enables you to define multiple network configurations for your development console and easily switch between them. This page describes the XNetConfig tool in the following sections.

  • Installing XNetConfig
  • Creating an XNetConfig .INI File
  • Using XNetConfig

Installing XNetConfig

The files for the XNetConfig tool reside in the (XDK Root)\xbox\bin\xnetconfig directory. To install them onto your development console, use XbCp to copy the files (including the Media directory) to the console's E: drive. When the copy is finished, XNetConfig will be available through the console's XDK Launcher program.

Creating an XNetConfig .INI File

When XNetConfig starts, it loads a file called XNetConfig.ini. You'll find a sample XNetConfig.ini file in the (XDK Root)\xbox\bin\xnetconfig directory.

The XNetConfig.ini file contains the various network settings that you want to select from. The settings are defined in groups called configurations. The sample XNetConfig.ini file has three configurations, called [Sample], [CorpNet], and [CableNet]. The labels for each of these configurations are followed by the groups of variables being set.

To make the configurations appear in the XNetConfig tool, you must list their names at the beginning of the XNetConfig.ini file in a section called [XNetConfig_Configs]. If a configuration's name does not appear in the [XNetConfig_Configs] section, it will not be listed and will not be selectable when you run XNetConfig.

The following variables can be set in a configuration.

Variable Name Comments
config_abEnet Spoofed MAC address.
config_ina Static IP address.
config_inaMask Static subnet mask.
config_inaGateway Static gateway address.
config_inaDnsServer1 Static address of DNS server 1.
config_inaDnsServer2 Static address of DNS server 2.
config_achDhcpHostName DHCP host name.
config_achPppoeUserName PPPoE user name.
config_achPppoePassword PPPoE password.
config_achPppoeServer PPPoE server.
config_achPppoeService PPPoE service.
debug_ina Static debug IP address.
debug_inaMask Static debug subnet mask.
debug_inaGateway Static debug gateway.
debug_inaDnsServer1 Static debug DNS server 1.
debug_inaDnsServer2 Static debug DNS server 2.

Using XNetConfig

When you start XNetConfig from the XDK Launcher, you are given the choice of either viewing the current configuration or loading a configuration defined in XNetConfig.ini.

If you choose to load a configuration, you'll be presented with the list of configurations defined in the [XNetConfig_Configs] of your XNetConfig.ini file. Select one by highlighting it and pressing the A button.

If you choose to view the current configuration, XNetConfig presents a list containing the following information.

Variable Name Comments
wFlags A set of flags that can be one of the following values.
Flag Description
XNCP_FLAGS_ETHERNET_SPOOF Use abEnet as spoofed ethernet address.
XNCP_FLAGS_IP_PPPOE Use PPPoE configuration of IP address, mask, and gateways.
XNCP_FLAGS_IP_STATIC Use manual configuration of IP address, mask, and gateways.
XNCP_FLAGS_DNS_STATIC Use manual configuration of the DNS servers.
XNCP_FLAGS_DHCP_HOST_NAME Send the host name to DHCP server.
abEnet Spoofed ethernet address.
ina Static IP address.
inaMask Static IP subnet mask.
inaGateway Static IP address of the default gateway.
ainaDnsServers Static IP addresses of the DNS servers.
achDhcpHostName Host name to send to the DHCP servers.
achPppoeUserName User name for PPPoE configuration.
achPppoePassword Password for PPPoE configuration.
achPppoeServer Server name for PPPoE configuration.
achPppoeService Service name for PPPoE configuration.
liTimeOfLease System time of last the DHCP lease (0 if no lease).
dwSecsInLease Time to lease expiration in seconds.
inaLease IP address of the DHCP lease.
inaMaskLease IP subnet mask of the DHCP lease.
inaServerLease IP address of DHCP server granting the lease.
ainaGatewaysLease IP addresses of the gateways configured by the DHCP lease.
ainaDnsServersLease IP addresses of the DNS servers configured by the DHCP lease.
abEnetUPnP Ethernet address of the last gateway checked for UPnP support.
wPortUPnP Port mapping assigned by the last gateway supporting UPnP.
